text:
Once upon a twilight-colored evening, there was an old oak tree. This wasn't any ordinary tree; it had seen empires rise and fall as well as winters. It had seen children hide from monsters.

One autumn evening, a young girl named Elara wandered into its shadow. She was searching for her grandmother's old bookshop. As she rested against the tree, listening to the whispers of leaves, she fell asleep.

In her dreams, Elara learned about the owl who spent winters here. The owl kept watch over treasures lost long ago. She heard about the hare who hid precious carrots. And there was the fox, clever as ever.

The next morning, the sunrise painted skies anew, and Elara awakened at grandma's shop. She learned that the old oak tree was guardian of stories, keeper of secrets. She planted a berry bush nearby.

Years passed. The oak grew stronger. Its branches became treasures themselves, shelters for tiny birds.
